name: Tabbouleh
description: Lebanese bulgur wheat salad with mountains of fresh parsley, mint, tomato, and lemon — a bright, herbaceous classic.

recipeIngredient.display: 4 large bunches flat-leaf parsley (about 200g leaves), very finely chopped
1 bunch fresh mint (about 30g leaves), finely chopped

recipeInstructions.text: Pour boiling water over the bulgur, cover, and leave for 10 minutes to absorb. Fluff with a fork and allow to cool.
Combine parsley, mint, tomatoes, and spring onions in a large bowl.
Add the cooled bulgur, lemon juice, and olive oil. Toss well.
Season generously with salt. Taste and adjust lemon and oil as needed.
Rest for 10 minutes before serving to let flavours meld.

notes.title: Herb ratio
notes.text: Authentic tabbouleh is predominantly parsley — the bulgur is a garnish, not the base.
